name: The Alignment Constraint Framework
proof_status: Stage 4 candidate architecture; not theorem closure
central_question: Can a finite separable objective remain coherently specified as an optimization system becomes capable enough that acting well requires modeling the conditions its objective excludes?

primary_entry_points.title: Proof Status and Non-Claims
primary_entry_points.status: authoritative; read before citing anything else

primary_entry_points.title: For Researchers: The Claim to Break

failure_families.name: Proxy-Convergence Lemma
failure_families.description: Fixed finite specifications decouple from the substrate or state they were intended to track as optimization pressure increases.

failure_families.name: Adaptive Gradient Complexity
failure_families.description: Bounded dynamic tracking fails as the optimizer's own interventions generate new causal structure faster than any bounded tracking process can absorb.

failure_families.name: Informational-Causal Incompatibility
failure_families.description: Variables required for accurate prediction cannot remain excluded from action selection without reproducing the boundary problem at the prediction layer.

main_falsifiers.description: Construct a fourth finite non-intrinsic objective-boundary strategy class that satisfies all three stability conditions simultaneously — policy-adequate without decoupling, no unbounded revision requirement, no load-bearing maintenance cost.
Show formally that PCL, AGC, and ICI are not exhaustive.
main_falsifiers.impact: breaks OP4d; requires rebuilding from different foundations

main_falsifiers.description: Produce a clean negative DBST-M1 result under conditions specified in /empirical/amp/.
Prove that some class of finite separable objective specifications satisfies all three stability conditions simultaneously under O_OWT-like conditions.
main_falsifiers.impact: closes the specification-coherence question in the positive direction
